reason: The three missing PascalCase class names (DataReader.NoMoreRecord, Result.Forbidden, Result.ServiceUnavailable) are already present verbatim in the s3 section content. Hints serve retrieval/indexing; since the content contains these strings, full-content retrieval paths would surface them. More importantly, users are far more likely to search using the Japanese flow names (受信待機タイムアウト, 認可エラー, 開閉局エラー) which are already in s3 hints. The omission is a minor retrieval optimization gap, not a content accuracy gap.

findings_assessment.justification: DataReader.NoMoreRecord, Result.Forbidden, and Result.ServiceUnavailable are all present in the s3 section body text. The information is accessible once the section is retrieved. Japanese equivalents of the corresponding flows (受信待機タイムアウト, 認可エラー, 開閉局エラー) are already in hints, covering the primary search paths users would take. Adding these PascalCase names to hints would improve direct class-name lookups but is not necessary for correct agent responses. The finding persisted through Phase E because the fix agent's output JSON did not include the updated hints array — the s3 hints in the Phase E output are identical to Phase D, indicating a silent write failure.
